articles.title: Plane Crash Middlefield Ohio: Pilot Killed After Single-Engine Plane Goes Down in Geauga County
articles.description: The circumstances surrounding the incident have not yet been released. An investigation is expected to determine the cause of the crash.

articles.title: Police arrest alleged kingpin behind multi-state burglary racket, aide also held
articles.description: Police arrest alleged kingpin behind multi-state burglary racket, aide also held

articles.title: Netherlands Join Brazil & Argentina In Elite List, Become 8th Team In The World To...
articles.description: Netherlands reached 100 FIFA World Cup goals with a 5-1 thrashing of Sweden, becoming the eighth nation to achieve the milestone.

articles.title: One dead, another critical after armed gang chases, shoots rivals in Jodhpur
articles.description: One dead, another critical after armed gang chases, shoots rivals in Jodhpur

articles.title: Four months after trial run, Jaipur gets 29 new e-buses
articles.description: Four months after trial run, Jaipur gets 29 new e-buses

articles.title: UP showcases GCC potential at MachineCon Summit in Goa
articles.description: UP showcases GCC potential at MachineCon Summit in Goa

articles.title: Rebel SP MPs will help NDA on passage of key bills: Rajbhar
articles.description: SBSP chief Om Prakash Rajbhar claims rebel SP MPs may form a separate group to back NDA on key bills; UP deputy CM Keshav Maurya dismisses it.

articles.title: Maximum in a day: 3 Indian-flagged ships safely cross Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: India News: NEW DELHI: Three Indian-flagged tankers - Desh Vaibhav, Desh Vibhor and Sanmar Herald - carrying 8.6 lakh tonne of crude oil, crossed Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Father's Day: 7 Hollywood Actors Whose Children Followed Them Into Showbiz
articles.description: For Father's Day, we revisit these Hollywood celebrities and famous dads have children who have also become actors. Many of them also shared screen space together, while some have played younger versions of their parents.

articles.title: Three-time Olympic canoeist David Hearn denies vandalizing Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool after arrest
articles.description: Former US Olympian David Hearn was arrested near the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, accused of damaging the recently renovated landmark. Hearn, a decorated canoeist, vehemently denies the allegations, stating he only touched a detached piece of liner. The incident occurred just before President Trump publicly condemned vandalism at the site.

articles.title: BTS’ Jungkook’s former classmate reveals why he was bullied in school
articles.description: The anecdote, which recently circulated online, paints a picture of a shy young Jungkook long before he debuted as BTS’ “Golden Maknae.”

articles.title: Maharashtra: 5 tourists drown at Ganpatipule beach in Ratnagiri
articles.description: Five tourists drowned and three were rescued after a group of eight from Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar fell into the sea at Ganpatipule Beach in Maharashtra's Ratnagiri district. The tragedy occurred as they reportedly misjudged sea conditions.

articles.title: Final nail in the coffin for Uddhav Thackeray's party? Amit Shah gives daring statement on Shiv Sena
articles.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah took a swipe at Uddhav Thackeray while addressing a public gathering in Kolhapur on June 20, as political turmoil deepened within Shiv Sena (UBT). Shah's remarks came after six of Shiv Sena (UBT)'s nine Lok Sabha MPs skipped a parliamentary meeting in Delhi and sought recognition as a separate group by writing to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla. The rebel MPs are expected to formally join the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, a move that would further strengthen the BJP-led NDA government at the Centre.

articles.title: ₹110 crore disbursed to 1.64 lakh farmers in Annamayya district
articles.description: ₹110 crore disbursed to 1.64 lakh farmers in Annamayya district under the ‘Annadatha Sukhibhava-PM Kisan’ programme.

articles.title: Bengal witnessing new freshness, moving to a brighter future: PM Modi
articles.description: PM Modi, in his first rally in West Bengal after BJP's historic win, said the state is seeing a 'new freshness'. At the 'Paschimbanga Divas' event, he stated that Bengal has broken its shackles and is moving towards a brighter future.

articles.title: Psychology says if you can't sleep until the trash is taken out, it may be your brain seeking closure
articles.description: People who can't sleep until the trash is taken out may be seeking mental closure, routine and a sense of control rather than simply keeping their homes clean.

articles.title: Scientists Discover Unusual Changes In Exploded Stars In Nearby Galaxy
articles.description: The findings were presented at the American Astronomical Society meeting in California.

articles.title: Lebanon says Israel-Hezbollah war toll tops 4,000 after 83 killed on Friday
articles.description: The ministry also raised the toll in Israeli attacks on Friday from 47 to a final toll of 83 martyrs and 141 wounded , most of them in south Lebanon but also in the country's east.

articles.title: RS poll: Cong pins hope on CM for action against ‘betrayal’
articles.description: Jharkhand Rajya Sabha polls: Congress alleges cross-voting, urges CM Hemant Soren to probe “betrayal,” says all 16 MLAs backed Pranav Jha.

articles.title: India beat Afghanistan by nine wickets, sweep ODI series 3-0
articles.description: India beat Afghanistan by nine wickets, sweep ODI series 3-0

articles.title: Lucky Day and Unlucky Day of the Week (June 22–28, 2026): Mercury in Cancer and Mars in Gemini Bring a Fast-Paced Week for Every Zodiac Sign
articles.description: Discover the lucky and unlucky days for every zodiac sign from June 22–28, 2026. Learn how Mercury in Cancer and Mars in Gemini influence your week before Mercury Retrograde and the Full Moon arrive (AI Image)

articles.title: Re-NEET 2026: Bengaluru Declares 200-Metre Restricted Zone Around Centres, Shops Shut
articles.description: To facilitate the smooth conduct of Re-NEET 2026, Bengaluru's police and district administration have established a 200-meter prohibited zone around examination centres.

articles.title: Katy Perry announces new single 'Watch It Burn', set for June 25 release
articles.description: Katy Perry has announced her new single, 'Watch It Burn', releasing on June 25. The singer unveiled the cover art and shared that the song explores themes of suppressed anger and confronting pain from a tough year, following her 2024 album '143'.

articles.title: Injury rules Hardik Pandya out of England series; Kohli awaits test
articles.description: Star all-rounder Hardik Pandya has been ruled out of the upcoming England series due to a leg strain. Star batter Virat Kohli also remains sidelined with an injury and is scheduled to undergo a fitness test ahead of the series.

articles.title: 'Foreign Degree Is Rs 1 Crore Debt': Is Studying Abroad Still Worth The Price?
articles.description: As Indian Rupee weakens against major global currencies, the cost of overseas education is soaring.

articles.title: Gladiator II failed because it lacked a moral core: Russell Crowe
articles.description: Russell Crowe believes Gladiator II failed to match the original's success because it lacked the "moral core" rooted in Maximus's journey to avenge his family. He said the filmmakers of the sequel didn't understand why the first film was a hit.

articles.title: 2006 Nimbalkar murder: Padamsinh Patil, 8 others acquitted by CBI court
articles.description: A CBI court acquitted all nine accused, including former NCP leader Padamsinh Patil, in the 2006 double murder case of Congress leader Pawanraje Nimbalkar and his driver, citing a lack of evidence after a lengthy trial.

articles.title: ISSF Jr World C'ship: India adds 5 medals; Sameer, Rohit win gold
articles.description: Indian shooters added five medals on day two of the ISSF Junior World Championship 2026. Sameer (25m Rapid Fire Pistol) and Rohit Kanyan (50m Rifle 3 Positions) clinched gold, while Prachi Gaikwad won a silver, taking India's tally to 8.

articles.title: Auditors and regulators must work in tandem to strengthen Financial reporting ecosystem: NFRA chief Nitin Gupta
articles.description: National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) Chairman Nitin Gupta emphasized the need for closer collaboration between regulators and the accounting profession to strengthen audit quality and financial reporting in India. Speaking at an ICAI-NFRA workshop in Pune, he highlighted auditors' expanding responsibilities amid digital transformation and AI adoption. Gupta stressed that strong corporate governance, reliable financial reporting, and credible audits are essential for sustaining India's economic growth. ICAI leaders also underscored continuous professional development and technology-driven skill enhancement.

articles.title: Why Ravichandran Ashwin missed San Francisco Unicorns' MLC 2026 opener?
articles.description: Veteran Indian spinner Ravichandran Ashwin missed the San Francisco Unicorns' MLC 2026 opener due to ongoing recovery from a knee injury. The setback had previously forced him to withdraw from the Big Bash League. Ashwin, who last played in July 2025, is focusing on regaining full fitness before returning to competitive cricket later in the tournament.

articles.title: Telecom body COAI announces new leadership, appoints Rahul Vatts as chairperson
articles.description: COAI, India's digital communications body, held its annual meeting, electing Rahul Vatts of Bharti Airtel as Chairperson and Dr. Ravi P. Gandhi of Reliance Jio as Vice-Chairperson for 2026-27. The organization emphasized the sector's crucial role in India's digital economy, highlighting the evolution of technologies like 5G Advanced and AI. COAI pledged to collaborate with stakeholders for trusted, secure, and future-ready infrastructure to boost economic growth.

articles.title: A $300-billion opportunity? Why Iran's Revolutionary Guards are watching US talks closely
articles.description: As Washington and Tehran pursue a deal, Iran's Revolutionary Guards could emerge stronger. Oil export waivers, reconstruction funds and business ties may expand IRGC influence, raising concerns for investors and Western governments.

articles.title: PM Modi in West Bengal to launch projects, release over ₹18,880 crore to farmers under PM-KISAN
articles.description: PM Narendra Modi is on a two-day visit to West Bengal, where he will launch development projects, release the 23rd PM-KISAN instalment, participate in International Yoga Day celebrations and attend a programme at Syama Prasad Mookerjee Port

articles.title: "5-Year Consensual Relation Can't Be Termed Rape": Allahabad Court's Big Order
articles.description: Court ruled that a long-term consensual relationship between two adults cannot be treated as rape merely because a promise to marry was not fulfilled

articles.title: Chaos at Flipkart Glam Up Fest as influencers fight over hampers; video viral
articles.description: The Flipkart Glam Up Fest held at Delhi’s Pragati Maidan on June 19 brought together beauty influencers from across the country.

articles.title: 'Kuch log...': Sanjay Raut counters Eknath Shinde's 'sher akela aata hai' remark with loyal dog post
articles.description: Political tensions escalate between Shiv Sena factions as Sanjay Raut fires back at Eknath Shinde's 'lion' jibe with a post calling some people disloyal dogs. This follows speculation of six Uddhav Thackeray camp MPs potentially defecting to Shinde's side. Raut announced action, including disqualification efforts, against the absent MPs, while Thackeray emotionally appealed to workers, vowing not to hand the party to 'thieves'.

articles.title: All About Trump's New Air Force One: A $400 Million Jet Gifted By Qatar
articles.description: The United States Air Force has introduced a new "bridge aircraft", a modified Boeing 747-8i, to support presidential travel amid delays in the long-term Air Force One replacement programme.

articles.title: Bihar court grants interim relief to educator Khan Sir in coaching institute firing case
articles.description: A Bihar court granted interim relief to popular educator Faisal Khan, known as 'Khan Sir', in a firing and vandalism case. The court issued a 'no coercive action' order until the next hearing. Police had registered an FIR against Khan Sir and two others following an incident at his coaching institute where two guards were arrested based on video evidence of aerial firing and vandalism.

articles.title: Bizarre robbery: TN Man blocks signal with shirt to stop train, snatches gold chain, gets caught
articles.description: Chennai Railway Police arrest a man who dangerously blocked a railway signal light with his shirt to stop the Nellai Express and rob a passenger.

articles.title: Cabinet meeting to be held in Coastal Karnataka, says Chief Minister
articles.description: Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar announces a Cabinet meeting in Coastal Karnataka to enhance tourism and resolve investment challenges.

articles.title: Sena not a plot of land one can put a name on: Shinde
articles.description: Contrary to speculations, the six breakaway Sena (UBT) MPs who have reportedly submitted a letter to the Lok Sabha speaker seeking a merger with the Shinde-led Sena did not turn up at the foundation day programme organised by the Sena

articles.title: Hailee Steinfeld and Josh Allen finally reveal baby daughter's name two months after keeping fans in suspense
articles.description: Hailee Steinfeld publicly revealed the name of her daughter, Harper Haize Allen, through a heartfelt letter published on her Substack newsletter Beau Society on June 19. In the letter, addressed directly to Harper, Steinfeld reflects on early motherhood with rawness and warmth, describing how time now moves in ounces and naps rather than months. She and husband Josh Allen welcomed Harper on April 2, less than a year after their Santa Barbara wedding in May 2025.

articles.title: Will Mumbai's BEST buses run today? Strike continues after talks fail, lakhs of commuters hit
articles.description: The strike severely affected public transport across Mumbai. Out of BEST's fleet of 2,766 buses, only 48 remained operational on Friday. Several buses that initially left depots were reportedly forced to return after incidents of stone-pelting and obstruction by striking workers.

articles.title: Brazilian President Takes Swipe At Neymar: "Working Remotely At FIFA WC"
articles.description: Brazilian star Neymar is "the first player in the world to be called up to the national side and be working remotely", Brazil President Luiz Inacio Lula de Silva joked.

articles.title: 'Best in history': Who does Lamine Yamal think is football's greatest?
articles.description: Yamal's comments came after Messi led defending champions Argentina to a 3-0 win over Saudi Arabia at Kansas City Stadium on Wednesday.

articles.title: Anthropic blackout episode shows that frontier AI needs global governance
articles.description: India, with one of the world’s largest AI user bases and vast amounts of data that contribute to the value and capabilities of global AI systems, is a critical stakeholder in this debate. It must play an active role in shaping the international regulatory framework that will govern AI's future

articles.title: Iran is the biggest winner from US truce push: Jefferies report
articles.description: Iran has emerged as the biggest beneficiary of the proposed US-Iran ceasefire framework, according to Jefferies, which says the deal could unlock billions of dollars in frozen funds and ease sanctions on Tehran. The brokerage also flagged rising AI exuberance, sticky inflation and growing market concentration as key themes shaping the global investment landscape.

articles.title: New West Bengal government clears all CBI sanction requests
articles.description: The government also restored the general consent for prosecution -- revoked by the then TMC regime in 2018 -- last week.

articles.title: 77-year-old dies as IIT graduate pushes him after parking spat in Noida
articles.description: A heated parking dispute between a 77-year-old retired bank employee and his 30-year-old IIT engineer neighbour in Noida tragically ended with the elder man's death. The elderly victim was allegedly pushed to the ground during the argument, sustaining injuries. He passed away hours later in the hospital, prompting police to register a case of culpable homicide not amounting to murder against the neighbour.

articles.title: Big 'expose' on Covid origins: Documents allege Biden's top medical adviser funded Wuhan lab
articles.description: Outgoing DNI Tulsi Gabbard releases declassified documents alleging Biden's chief medical adviser funded Wuhan lab research, influenced Covid origin assessments and misled Congress under oath.
